AlgebraicKernelForSpheres.name: AlgebraicKernelForSpheres
AlgebraicKernelForSpheres.brief: The AlgebraicKernelForSpheres concept is meant to provide the curved kernel with all the algebraic functionalities required for the manipulation of spheres, circles, and circular arcs in 3D.
AlgebraicKernelForSpheres.detailed: Has models CGAL::Algebraic_kernel_for_spheres_2_3 SphericalKernel CGAL::Spherical_kernel_3 <Kernel, AlgebraicKernelForSpheres >

AlgebraicKernelForSpheres::PolynomialFor.brief: Concept to represent trivariate polynomials of degree up to 2 capable of storing equations of spheres, whose center's coordinates, as well as the square of the radius, are of a type that is a model of the concept FieldNumberType .

AlgebraicKernelForSpheres::RootForSphere.name: AlgebraicKernelForSpheres::RootForSpheres_2_3
AlgebraicKernelForSpheres::RootForSphere.brief: Concept to represent the roots of a system of three equations of degree 2 in three variables x , y and z that are models of concept AlgebraicKernelForSpheres::PolynomialForSpheres_2_3 .

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Copies in the output iterator the common roots of p1 , p2 , and p3 , with their multiplicity, as objects of type std::pair< AlgebraicKernelForSpheres::Root_for_spheres_2_3 , int> .
members.detailed: Here, Type1 , Type2 , and Type3 can all be either AlgebraicKernelForSpheres::Polynomial_1_3 or AlgebraicKernelForSpheres::Polynomial_for_spheres_2_3 . The set of solutions of the system is 0-dimensional.
members.argsstring: (const Type1 &p1, const Type2 &p2, const Type3 &p3, OutputIterator res)

CGAL::Circular_arc_3.name: CGAL::Circular_arc_3
CGAL::Circular_arc_3.detailed: Is model of SphericalKernel::CircularArc_3 I/O The input/output format of a circular arc consists of the supporting circle represented as a Circle_3 object followed by the source and target points of the arc represented as two Circular_arc_point_3 objects. The defined arc is the unique arc constructed from such three objects. CGAL::Circular_arc_point_3 < SphericalKernel > CGAL::Line_arc_3 < SphericalKernel >

members.name: Circular_arc_3
members.brief: Constructs an arc from a full circle.

members.name: Circular_arc_3
members.brief: Constructs an arc from a full circle, using pt as source and target.

members.name: Circular_arc_3
members.brief: The circular arc constructed from a circle, a source, and a target, is defined as the set of points of the circle that lie between the source p1 and the target p2 , when traversing the circle counterclockwise seen from the side of the plane of the circle pointed by its positive normal vectors.
members.detailed: In this definition, we say that a normal vector $ (a,b,c)$ is positive if $ (a,b,c)>(0,0,0)$ (i.e. $ (a>0) || (a==0) \&\& (b>0) || (a==0)\&\&(b==0)\&\&(c>0)$ ). Constructs the circular arc supported by c , whose source and target are p and q , respectively. p and q lie on c and are different.
members.argsstring: (const Circle_3< SphericalKernel > &c, const Circular_arc_point_3< SphericalKernel > &p, const Circular_arc_point_3< SphericalKernel > &q)

members.name: Circular_arc_3
members.brief: Constructs an arc that is supported by the circle of type Circle_3< SphericalKernel > passing through the points p , q and r .
members.detailed: The source and target are respectively p and r , when traversing the supporting circle in the counterclockwise direction seen from the side of the plane containing the circle pointed by its positive normal vectors. Note that, depending on the orientation of the point triple (p,q,r) , q may not lie on the arc. p , q , and r are not collinear.
members.argsstring: (const Point_3< SphericalKernel > &p, const Point_3< SphericalKernel > &q, const Point_3< SphericalKernel > &r)

members.name: center
members.brief: returns the center of the supporting circle.

members.name: squared_radius
members.brief: returns the squared radius of the supporting circle.

members.name: operator==
members.brief: Test for equality.
members.detailed: Two arcs are equal, iff their non-oriented supporting planes are equal, and the centers and squared radii of their respective supporting circles are equal, and their sources and targets are equal.

members.name: operator!=
members.brief: Test for nonequality.

CGAL::Circular_arc_point_3.detailed: Is model of SphericalKernel::CircularArcPoint_3 CGAL::Circular_arc_3 < SphericalKernel > CGAL::Line_arc_3 < SphericalKernel >

members.name: x
members.brief: $ x$ -coordinate of the point.

members.name: y
members.brief: $ y$ -coordinate of the point.

members.name: z
members.brief: $ z$ -coordinate of the point.

members.name: bbox
members.brief: Returns a bounding box around the point.

members.name: operator==
members.brief: Test for equality.
members.detailed: Two points are equal, iff their $ x$ , $ y$ and $ z$ coordinates are equal.

members.name: operator!=
members.brief: Test for nonequality.

members.name: operator<
members.brief: Returns true iff p is lexicographically smaller than q , i.e. either if p.x() < q.x() or if p.x() == q.x() and p.y() < q.y() or if p.x() == q.x() and p.y() == q.y() and p.z() < q.z() .

members.name: operator>
members.brief: Returns true iff p is lexicographically greater than q .

members.name: operator<=
members.brief: Returns true iff p is lexicographically smaller than or equal to q .

members.name: operator>=
members.brief: Returns true iff p is lexicographically greater than or equal to q .

CGAL::Exact_spherical_kernel_3.name: CGAL::Exact_spherical_kernel_3
CGAL::Exact_spherical_kernel_3.brief: A typedef to a spherical kernel that provides both exact geometric predicates and exact geometric constructions.
CGAL::Exact_spherical_kernel_3.detailed: It defines the same types as CGAL::Spherical_kernel_3 . Is model of SphericalKernel

CGAL::Line_arc_3.name: CGAL::Line_arc_3
CGAL::Line_arc_3.detailed: Is model of SphericalKernel::LineArc_3 CGAL::Circular_arc_point_3 < SphericalKernel > CGAL::Circular_arc_3 < SphericalKernel >

members.name: Line_arc_3
members.brief: Construct the line segment supported by l , whose source is p1 , and whose target is p2 .
members.detailed: p1 and p2 lie on l . p1 and p2 are different.
members.argsstring: (const Line_3< SphericalKernel > &l, const Circular_arc_point_3< SphericalKernel > &p1, const Circular_arc_point_3< SphericalKernel > &p2)

members.name: Line_arc_3
members.brief: Same.
members.argsstring: (const Line_3< SphericalKernel > &l, const Point_3< SphericalKernel > &p1, const Point_3< SphericalKernel > &p2)

members.name: min
members.brief: Constructs the minimum vertex according to the lexicographic ordering of coordinates.

members.name: max
members.brief: Same for the maximum vertex.

members.name: is_vertical
members.brief: Returns true iff the segment is vertical.

members.name: operator==
members.brief: Test for equality.
members.detailed: Two segments are equal, iff their non-oriented supporting lines are equal (i.e. they define the same set of points), and their endpoints are the same.

members.name: operator!=
members.brief: Test for nonequality.

members.name: operator>>
members.brief: The format for input/output is, for each line arc: a Line_3 (the supporting line) and two Circular_arc_point_3 (the two endpoints), under the condition that the endpoints are actually lying on the line.

members.name: operator<<
members.brief: The format for input/output is, for each line arc: a Line_3 (the supporting line) and two Circular_arc_point_3 (the two endpoints), under the condition that the endpoints are actually lying on the line.

CGAL::Spherical_kernel_3.name: CGAL::Spherical_kernel_3
CGAL::Spherical_kernel_3.detailed: Is model of SphericalKernel Parameters The first parameter of the spherical kernel must be instantiated with a model of the Kernel concept. The Spherical_kernel_3 class template derives from this first parameter, in order to reuse all needed functionalities on basic objects provided by a model of the Kernel concept. The second parameter, AlgebraicKernelForSpheres , is meant to provide the spherical kernel with all the algebraic functionalities required for the manipulation of algebraic curves. Kernel AlgebraicKernelForSpheres CGAL::Exact_spherical_kernel_3

SphericalKernel::CompareTheta_3.detailed: SphericalKernel::CompareThetaZ_3 SphericalKernel::CompareX_3 SphericalKernel::CompareY_3 SphericalKernel::CompareZ_3 SphericalKernel::CompareXY_3 SphericalKernel::CompareXYZ_3 SphericalKernel::Equal_3

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Compares the $ \theta$ -coordinates of p and q in the cylindrical coordinate system relative to the context sphere used by the function SphericalKernel::compare_theta_3_object .
members.detailed: p and q lie on the context sphere used by the function SphericalKernel::compare_theta_3_object , but do not coincide with its poles.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Compares the $ \theta$ -coordinates of p and of the meridian defined by m (see Section Spherical Kernel Objects ) in the cylindrical coordinate system relative to the context sphere used by the function SphericalKernel::compare_theta_3_object .
members.detailed: p lies on the context sphere used by the function SphericalKernel::compare_theta_3_object , but does not coincide with its poles. m $ \neq(0,0,0)$ and the $ z$ -coordinate of m is $ 0$ .

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Same as previous, with opposite result.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Compares the $ \theta$ -coordinates of the meridians defined by m1 and by m2 (see Section Spherical Kernel Objects ) in the cylindrical coordinate system relative to the context sphere used by the function SphericalKernel::compare_theta_3_object .
members.detailed: m1 $ \neq(0,0,0)$ , m2 $ \neq(0,0,0)$ and the $ z$ -coordinate of m1 and m2 is $ 0$ .

SphericalKernel::CompareZ_3.name: SphericalKernel::CompareZ_3
SphericalKernel::CompareZ_3.detailed: Refines Kernel::CompareZ_3 SphericalKernel::CompareX_3 SphericalKernel::CompareY_3 SphericalKernel::CompareXY_3 SphericalKernel::CompareXYZ_3 SphericalKernel::CompareTheta_3 SphericalKernel::CompareThetaZ_3 SphericalKernel::Equal_3

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Compares the $ z$ -coordinates of p and q .

members.name: operator()
members.brief: compares the $ z$ -coordinates of the two intersections points of a0 and a1 with the meridian defined by m (see Section Spherical Kernel Objects ).
members.detailed: a0 and a1 lie on the context sphere used by the function SphericalKernel::compare_z_at_theta_3_object . m $ \neq(0,0,0)$ and the $ z$ -coordinate of m is $ 0$ . Arcs a0 and a1 are $ \theta$ -monotone and both intersected by the meridian defined by m (see Section Spherical Kernel Objects ).

members.name: operator()
members.brief: given a meridian anchored at the poles of the context sphere used by the function SphericalKernel::compare_z_at_theta_3_object , and passing through point p , compares the $ z$ -coordinate of point p and that of the intersection of the meridian with a .
members.detailed: a and p lie on the context sphere used by the function SphericalKernel::compare_z_at_theta_3_object , arc a is $ \theta$ -monotone and the meridian passing through p intersects arc a .

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Compares the $ z$ -coordinates of the intersection points of both arcs with a meridian anchored at the poles of the context sphere used by the function SphericalKernel::compare_z_to_right_3_object , at a $ \theta$ -coordinate infinitesimally greater that the $ \theta$ -coordinate of point p .
members.detailed: a0 and a1 lie on the context sphere used by the function SphericalKernel::compare_z_to_right_3_object , a0 and a1 are $ \theta$ -monotone, p lies on a0 and a1 and is not a $ \theta$ -extremal point of the supporting circle of a0 or a1 .

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Computes an approximation of the angle of the arc in radians a .

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Computes an approximation of the squared length of the arc a .

SphericalKernel::ConstructCircularArc_3.name: SphericalKernel::ConstructCircularArc_3
SphericalKernel::ConstructCircularArc_3.brief: The circular arc constructed from a circle, a source, and a target, is defined as the set of points of the circle that lie between the source p1 and the target p2 , when traversing the circle counterclockwise seen from the side of the plane of the circle pointed by its positive normal vectors.
SphericalKernel::ConstructCircularArc_3.detailed: In this definition, we say that a normal vector $ (a,b,c)$ is positive if $ (a,b,c)>(0,0,0)$ (i.e. $ (a>0) || (a==0) \&\& (b>0) || (a==0)\&\&(b==0)\&\&(c>0)$ ).

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Constructs an arc from a full circle.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Constructs the circular arc supported by c , whose source and target are p and q , respectively.
members.detailed: p and q lie on c and they are different.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Constructs an arc that is supported by the circle of type SphericalKernel::Circle_3 passing through the points p , q and r .
members.detailed: The source and target are respectively p and r , when traversing the supporting circle in the counterclockwise direction seen from the side of the plane containing the circle pointed by its positive normal vectors. the circle. Note that, depending on the orientation of the point triple (p,q,r) , q may not lie on the arc. p , q , and r are not collinear.

members.brief: For two arcs.
members.detailed: Two arcs are equal, iff their non-oriented supporting planes are equal, and the centers and squared radii of their respective supporting circles are equal, and their sources and targets are equal.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: For two segments.
members.detailed: Two segments are equal, iff their non-oriented supporting lines are equal (i.e. they define the same set of points), and their endpoints are the same.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Copies in the output iterator the intersection elements between the two objects.
members.detailed: intersections iterates on elements of type CGAL::Object , in lexicographic order when this ordering is defined on the computed objects. Type1 and Type2 can both be either: SphericalKernel::Sphere_3 , SphericalKernel::Plane_3 , SphericalKernel::Line_3 , SphericalKernel::Circle_3 , SphericalKernel::Line_arc_3 or SphericalKernel::Circular_arc_3 depending on the types Type1 and Type2, the computed CGAL::Object 's can be assigned to std::pair< SphericalKernel::Circular_arc_point_3 , unsigned>, where the unsigned integer is the multiplicity of the corresponding intersection point between obj_1 and obj_2, Type1, when Type1 and Type2 are equal, and if the two objects obj1 and obj2 are equal, SphericalKernel::Line_3 or SphericalKernel::Circle_3 when Type1 and Type2 are two-dimensional objects intersecting along a curve (2 planes, or 2 spheres, or one plane and one sphere), SphericalKernel::Circular_arc_3 in case of an overlap of two circular arcs or SphericalKernel::Line_arc_3 in case of an overlap of two line segments.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Copies in the output iterator the intersection elements between the three objects.
members.detailed: intersections iterates on elements of type CGAL::Object , in lexicographic order when this ordering is defined on the computed objects. Type1, Type2 and Type3 can be either: SphericalKernel::Sphere_3 or SphericalKernel::Plane_3 and depending of these types, the computed CGAL::Object 's can be assigned to std::pair< SphericalKernel::Circular_arc_point_3 , unsigned>, where the unsigned integer is the multiplicity of the corresponding intersection point, SphericalKernel::Circle_3 or Type1, when Type1, Type2 and Type3 are equal, and if the three objects obj1 and obj2 and obj3 are equal.
members.argsstring: (const Type1 &obj1, const Type2 &obj2, const Type3 &obj3, OutputIterator intersections)

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Tests whether the arc a is $ \theta$ -monotone, i.e. the intersection of any meridian anchored at the poles of the context sphere used by the function SphericalKernel::is_theta_monotone_3_object and the arc a is reduced to at most one point in general, and two points if a pole of that sphere is an endpoint of a .
members.detailed: Note that a bipolar circle has no such arcs. a lies on the context sphere used by the function SphericalKernel::is_theta_monotone_3_object , and the supporting circle of a is not bipolar.

SphericalKernel::LineArc_3.name: SphericalKernel::LineArc_3
SphericalKernel::LineArc_3.brief: Concept for line segments supported by a line that is a model of Kernel::Line_3 , and whose endpoints are models of the SphericalKernel::CircularArcPoint_3 concept.
SphericalKernel::LineArc_3.detailed: Refines CopyConstructible Assignable DefaultConstructible Has models CGAL::Line_arc_3 < SphericalKernel >

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Copies in the output iterator the results of the split of arc a at the $ \theta$ -extremal point(s) of its supporting circle relatively to the context sphere used by the function SphericalKernel::make_theta_monotone_3_object (Refer to Section Spherical Kernel Objects for the definition of these points.) The output iterator may contain no arc (if the supporting circle is a bipolar circle), one arc (if a is already $ \theta$ -monotone), two arcs (if only one $ \theta$ -extremal point is on a ), or three arcs (if two $ \theta$ -extremal points are on a ).
members.detailed: a lies on the context sphere used by the function SphericalKernel::make_theta_monotone_3_object , and the supporting circle of a is not bipolar.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Copies in the output iterator the results of the split of circle c at its $ \theta$ -extremal point(s) relatively to the context sphere used by the function SphericalKernel::make_theta_monotone_3_object .
members.detailed: (Refer to Section Spherical Kernel Objects for the definition of these points.) The output iterator may contain no arc (if the circle is bipolar), one arc (if the circle is polar or threaded), or two arcs (if the circle is normal). The source and target are such that the circular arc is the set of points of the circle that lie between the source and the target when traversing the circle counterclockwise seen from the positive side of the plane of the circle. In this definition, we say that a normal vector $ (a,b,c)$ is positive if $ (a>0) || (a==0) \&\& (b>0) || (a==0)\&\&(b==0)\&\&(c>0)$ . For a threaded circle, the arc returned the one built using the full circle. For a polar circle, the arc returned is the full circle, the source and target correspond to the pole the circle goes through. c lies on the context sphere used by the function SphericalKernel::make_theta_monotone_3_object , and c is not bipolar.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Splits arc a at point p , which creates arcs a1 and a2 .
members.detailed: The point p lies in the interior of the input arc a .

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Same for a line arc.

members.name: operator==
members.brief: Test for equality.
members.detailed: Two points are equal, iff their $ x$ , $ y$ and $ z$ coordinates are equal.

members.name: operator!=
members.brief: Test for nonequality.

members.name: operator<
members.brief: Returns true iff p is lexicographically smaller than q , i.e. either if p.x() < q.x() or if p.x() == q.x() and p.y() < q.y() or if p.x() == q.x() and p.y() == q.y() and p.z() < q.z() .

members.name: operator>
members.brief: Returns true iff p is lexicographically greater than q .

members.name: operator<=
members.brief: Returns true iff p is lexicographically smaller than or equal to q .

members.name: operator>=
members.brief: Returns true iff p is lexicographically greater than or equal to q .

members.brief: Compares the $ \theta$ -coordinates of p and of the meridian defined by m (see Section Spherical Kernel Objects ) in the cylindrical coordinate system relative to sphere .
members.detailed: p lies on sphere , but does not coincide with its poles. m $ \neq(0,0,0)$ and the $ z$ -coordinate of m is $ 0$ . members.brief: Compares the $ \theta$ -coordinates of the meridian defined by m and of p (see Section Spherical Kernel Objects ) in the cylindrical coordinate system relative to sphere .

members.brief: Compares p and q according to the lexicographic ordering on $ \theta$ and $ z$ -coordinates in the cylindrical coordinate system relative to sphere .
members.detailed: p and q lie on sphere , but do not coincide with the poles of sphere . CGAL::compare_x() CGAL::compare_xy() CGAL::compare_xy() CGAL::compare_x_at_y() CGAL::compare_y() CGAL::compare_yx() CGAL::compare_y_at_x() CGAL::compare_z() CGAL::compare_theta()

members.name: is_theta_monotone
members.brief: Tests whether the arc a is $ \theta$ -monotone, i.e. the intersection of any meridian anchored at the poles sphere and the arc a is reduced to at most one point in general, and two points if a pole of sphere is an endpoint of the arc.
members.detailed: Note that a bipolar circle has no such arcs. a lies on sphere , and the supporting circle of a is not bipolar.

members.name: theta_extremal_point
members.brief: Returns the point on the circle that is extremal in $ \theta$ using the cylindrical coordinate system relative to sphere , and that has the smallest (resp. largest) $ \theta$ -coordinate of the two points if b is true (resp. false ).
members.detailed: See Section Spherical Kernel Objects for definitions. c lies on sphere and is a normal circle.
members.argsstring: (const CGAL::Circle_3< SphericalKernel > &c, const CGAL::Sphere_3< SphericalKernel > sphere, bool b)

members.name: theta_extremal_points
members.brief: Copies in the output iterator the $ \theta$ -extremal points of the circle relatively to sphere .
members.detailed: res iterates on elements of type Circular_arc_point_3 < SphericalKernel > , lexicographically sorted in the cylindrical coordinate system relative to sphere . See Section Spherical Kernel Objects for definitions. c lies on sphere and is a normal circle.
members.argsstring: (const CGAL::Circle_3< SphericalKernel > &c, const CGAL::Sphere_3< SphericalKernel > &sphere, OutputIterator res)

members.name: x_extremal_point
members.brief: Returns the point on the sphere that is extremal in the $ x$ -direction, and that is the smallest (resp. largest) of the two $ x$ -extremal points for the lexicographic order if b is true (resp. false ).

PkgCircularKernel3Ref.name: PkgCircularKernel3Ref
PkgCircularKernel3Ref.detailed: Pedro Machado Manhães de Castro, Frédéric Cazals, Sébastien Loriot, and Monique Teillaud This package is an extension of the linear CGAL Kernel. It offers functionalities on spheres, circles, circular arcs and line segments, in the 3D space or restricted on a reference sphere.
